description_long: This object is a compact submachine gun-style rifle, reminiscent of the iconic MAC-10, with a boxy rectangular receiver and a prominent magazine extending downward from the grip. The body is predominantly matte black, with minimal surface detailing except for a few ridges and a cylindrical charging handle on the upper left side. The trigger guard is squared, and the grip is straight and utilitarian, designed for one-handed or two-handed operation. A wire-style folding stock is attached to the rear, currently in the folded position, and a short barrel protrudes from the front. The magazine is long and slender, likely designed for high-capacity use. The overall design is utilitarian, emphasizing compactness and ease of handling in close-quarters urban environments.
description: A compact, matte black submachine gun-style rifle with a boxy receiver, folding wire stock, and extended magazine. Its utilitarian design is suited for close-quarters urban use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, including the folded wire stock, charging handle, and extended magazine.
description_view_1: Front-on view showing the narrow profile of the rifle, with the barrel and magazine aligned vertically.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, highlighting the wire stock, magazine, and minimal surface features.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the receiver, folded stock, and the alignment of the grip and magazine.
